title: Crepidomanes mettenii
taxonomy: Crepidomanes mettenii is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This fern is commonly found in the tropical regions of Southeast Asia, where it thrives in humid environments. It belongs to the genus Crepidomanes, which consists of over 200 species of air plants. The family Hymenophyllaceae is a group of small to medium-sized ferns, mostly epiphytic, that are widely distributed across the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The classification of Crepidomanes mettenii reflects its unique adaptations and ecological niches within its native habitats.

intro: Crepidomanes mettenii, also known as Metten's creeping fern, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
